name: WP-secapi-dos-race
description: Close one DoS vector and three race/atomicity defects: add a negative cache + cooldown to the unauthenticated JWKS forced re-fetch (F-181, HIGH); make same-day dictation writes non-destructive (F-120, data-loss); enforce CAS on the optimistic-concurrency version check in updateInterestsContext (F-164); wrap the regenerate ownership-check->delete-all->insert sequence in a transaction (F-167).
acceptanceCriteria: F-181/120/164/167 each remediated with proof; concurrency negative-path tests for the CAS + transaction fixes; JWKS cooldown/negative-cache verified.
riskImpact: P1: unauthenticated JWKS forced re-fetch is a DoS amplifier; the three race/atomicity defects cause data loss (dictation overwrite), lost updates (interests CAS), and partial-write corruption (regenerate) under concurrency.
bodyText:
Bundle brief — DoS + race/atomicity
Rationale: one unauthenticated DoS amplifier and three concurrency defects threaten availability and data integrity. Strategy: bound the JWKS forced re-fetch with a negative cache + cooldown; make concurrent writes safe via non-destructive upsert, compare-and-set on the optimistic version, and a transaction around the regenerate sequence. Scope: the JWKS fetcher + the three write paths; one PR.
F-181 (HIGH) — negative cache + cooldown on unauthenticated JWKS forced re-fetch
F-120 — same-day dictation writes made non-destructive (data-loss)
F-164 — enforce CAS on the optimistic-concurrency version check in updateInterestsContext
F-167 — wrap regenerate ownership-check→delete-all→insert in a transaction
Acceptance Criteria: F-181/120/164/167 each remediated with proof; concurrency negative-path tests for the CAS + transaction fixes; JWKS cooldown/negative-cache verified. Children 709/710 are absorbed provenance; lands as one PR.
